name: Foxhole/Fighting Position
prompt:
Vietnam War era individual fighting position (foxhole). Dug-in infantry position for one or two soldiers.

Physical structure:
- Hole: oval-shaped excavation, ~1.8m x 1.2m, ~1.2m deep
  - Dirt walls sloping slightly inward
  - Flat bottom
  - Shown as depression below ground level (Y=0 is ground)
- Parapet: mound of excavated dirt around rim
  - Higher at front (toward enemy), ~0.3m above ground
  - Lower at rear for entry
  - Irregular earth mound
- Sandbags: 3-4 sandbags placed on front parapet for extra protection
- Firing step: small ledge inside at front wall, ~0.5m from bottom
  - Where soldier stands to fire over parapet
- Grenade sump: small deeper hole in corner of floor (drain for grenades)
  - Just a darker patch
- Overhead cover: optional poncho/canvas stretched over rear half (simplified as flat angled panel)

Colors: Dirt walls (0x5a4a30), dirt parapet (0x6B5530), sandbags (0xc2a878), ground surface (0x4a5a2a), canvas cover (0x6B6B4F)
Orientation: Front (parapet) facing +Z, at ground level
Scale: Real-world meters (~1.8m x 1.2m, ~1.5m deep including parapet)
